title: Recognizing the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, commending a month of fasting and spiritual renewal, and extending best wishes to Muslims in the United States and across the globe for a joyous and meaningful observance of Eid al-Fitr.
plain_summary: This congressional resolution celebrates the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, which is a time when Muslims fast and focus on their spiritual growth. The House of Representatives recognizes the important contributions that American Muslims have made to the country. The resolution also celebrates Eid al-Fitr, which is the happy holiday that marks the end of Ramadan. Finally, it sends best wishes to all Muslims in America and around the world during this special time.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution recognizes the Muslim holy month of Ramadan, praises a month of fasting and spiritual renewal, and sends best wishes to Muslims in the United States and around the world for a happy and meaningful celebration of Eid al-Fitr (the holiday that ends Ramadan).

sections.title: Background Facts
sections.text: Ramadan is the holy month when Muslims around the world fast and focus on spiritual growth. This month of sacrifice and thinking deeply began on February twenty-eighth, two thousand twenty-five, based on when people could see the crescent moon in the sky. Muslims in the United States and around the world observe Ramadan by not eating during daylight hours, practicing self-control, worshiping, giving to charity, and reading the Holy Quran (their sacred book) to become more patient, humble, and spiritual. Ramadan continued until the celebration of Eid al-Fitr. Eid al-Fitr begins each year when people see the new moon, which means the month-long period of fasting and reflection is over for all Muslim followers. There are about one billion nine hundred million Muslims worldwide. Over nine thousand five hundred fourteen Muslims serve on active duty and over six thousand six hundred twenty-two Muslims serve as selected reserve personnel in the United States Armed Forces. There are over two hundred elected officials who identify as Muslim. There are an estimated one million two hundred thousand Muslim voters in the United States. The Muslim population has been growing in the United States and today's American Muslim population includes people from many different ethnic, racial, language, social, and economic backgrounds. American Muslims have contributed to every part of society and made important advances in medicine, science, engineering, economics, sports, education, and law. The observance of the Muslim holy month of Ramadan has ended with Eid al-Fitr, a three-day celebration.

sections.title: House Decision
sections.text: The House of Representatives decides that it shows solidarity with and support for members of the Islamic community in the United States and throughout the world, and the House of Representatives recognizes the importance of the Islamic faith. In observance of and out of respect for Ramadan tradition, the Muslim holy month of fasting and spiritual renewal, the House offers its best wishes to all Muslims celebrating Eid al-Fitr, the end of Ramadan, while also expressing its deepest respect to Muslims in the United States and worldwide on this important occasion.
